name: expo-dev-client
description: Build and distribute Expo development clients locally or via TestFlight
capabilities: Generate EAS Build configuration for development clients
Build iOS development clients for TestFlight distribution
Build iOS and Android development clients locally
Install development clients on simulators and physical devices
Troubleshoot common build and signing issues
Configure development profiles for different platforms
author: expo
securitySummary: All 38 static findings evaluated as false positives. The skill contains legitimate documentation for Expo EAS CLI commands and standard development workflows. No malicious patterns, command injection vectors, or data exfiltration detected.
valueStatement: Expo Go does not support custom native code. This skill helps you build custom development clients for testing native modules, widgets, and app extensions on physical devices.
limitations: Does not create new Expo projects from scratch
Does not handle Apple Developer program setup or certificates
Does not modify existing native code in your project
Requires an active Expo account and EAS CLI

useCases.targetUser: React Native developers adding custom native functionality
useCases.title: Test custom native modules
useCases.description: Create a development client to test third-party native modules that are not supported in Expo Go. Use this when you need to iterate on native code changes.

useCases.targetUser: iOS developers building Apple platform extensions
useCases.title: Build for iOS widgets and extensions
useCases.description: Create a development client that supports Apple widgets, app clips, and extensions. Required for testing iOS-specific platform features.

useCases.title: Distribute test builds via TestFlight
useCases.description: Build a development client and submit it to TestFlight for QA testing on physical devices without App Store review.

promptTemplates.title: Quick development client setup
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to create a development client for my Expo project. My app uses custom native code that does not work in Expo Go. Help me set up the eas.json configuration and build a development client for iOS.
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up EAS configuration for the first time

promptTemplates.title: Build for TestFlight
promptTemplates.prompt: Build and submit my iOS development client to TestFlight so my QA team can test on physical devices. What commands do I need to run and what happens after the build is ready?
promptTemplates.scenario: Distributing test builds to external testers

promptTemplates.title: Local build troubleshooting
promptTemplates.prompt: My local iOS build is failing with signing errors. Help me diagnose the issue and configure my credentials correctly using eas credentials.

promptTemplates.title: Android development build
promptTemplates.prompt: Build an Android development client locally. I want to install it on a physical device for testing. Walk me through the process from eas.json configuration to adb installation.

bestPractices: Start with Expo Go to validate your JavaScript code before building a development client
Use TestFlight distribution for team testing to avoid device-side installation complexity
Increment build numbers automatically using 'autoIncrement: true' in eas.json
antiPatterns: Creating a development client when Expo Go would suffice
Skipping the eas credentials configuration when build errors occur
Using local builds for team distribution instead of TestFlight

faq.question: When should I use a development client instead of Expo Go?
faq.answer: Use a development client when your app requires custom native code, such as local Expo modules, Apple widgets, app clips, extensions, or third-party native modules not supported by Expo Go.

faq.question: How do I install a local iOS build on my device?
faq.answer: Extract the build with 'tar -xzf build-*.tar.gz', then use Xcode Devices window to install the .app file, or use 'ideviceinstaller -i build.ipa' for physical devices.

faq.question: Can I build for both iOS and Android in one command?
faq.answer: Yes. Run 'eas build --profile development' without specifying a platform to build for both iOS and Android simultaneously.

faq.question: How do I check the status of my EAS build?
faq.answer: Use 'eas build:list' to see recent builds and 'eas build:view' for detailed information about a specific build.

faq.question: What is the difference between local and cloud builds?
faq.answer: Cloud builds run on EAS infrastructure and support TestFlight submission. Local builds run on your machine, require Xcode/Android Studio, and produce files you install manually.

faq.question: How do I connect the dev client to my local Metro bundler?
faq.answer: Run 'npx expo start --dev-client' to start the bundler. The dev client will show a QR code scanner or allow you to enter your local server URL manually.
